content: PD diagnosis uses MDS Clinical Diagnostic Criteria (2015). Core: bradykinesia plus rest tremor and/or rigidity. Supportive criteria: clear benefit from dopaminergic therapy, levodopa-induced dyskinesia, rest tremor of limb, hyposmia, abnormal cardiac MIBG scintigraphy. Exclusion criteria: cerebellar signs, downward vertical gaze palsy, normal functional neuroimaging of presynaptic dopamine transporters (DAT-SPECT/PET), documented alternative cause. Probable PD requires no exclusion criteria and ≥2 supportive features. Definite PD requires pathological confirmation. DAT-SPECT shows reduced striatal dopamine transporter uptake but cannot distinguish PD from atypical parkinsonism. Red flags suggesting atypical parkinsonism: early falls, rapid progression, poor levodopa response, early autonomic failure (suggests MSA), vertical gaze palsy (PSP), cortical sensory loss (CBD), cerebellar signs. Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (UPDRS/MDS-UPDRS) quantifies motor and non-motor symptoms for monitoring. Hoehn and Yahr staging describes disease progression (stage 1: unilateral; stage 3: bilateral with balance impairment; stage 5: wheelchair/bedbound).
